name: MILELRS
description: Military fork of ExpressLRS 3.3.2. Adds multiband redundancy (900 MHz + 2.4 GHz simultaneous), encrypted per-transmitter binding keys, real-time EW spectrum scanning with direction-finding, automatic channel switching on interference detection, multi-RX support for redundant control paths, swarm ID, and extended failsafe timers. Pairs with MILBETA for OSD tactical display. Most widely deployed military control link in the Russo-Ukrainian War.

name: MILBETA
description: Military fork of Betaflight (4.3.3/4.5.1). Processes tactical data from MILELRS receivers and displays on pilot OSD. OSD camouflage, EW direction-finding overlay, VTX frequency unlock (3000-6999 MHz), extended failsafe timers (10x), LQ statistics across 12 bands, armed VTX switching.

name: BarvinokLRS
description: Independent Ukrainian control link firmware (not an ELRS fork). Distributed free to frontline military units. Operates on multiple frequencies with different RF parameters. Field-proven to maintain link under EW conditions where other systems fail. Defense Builder accelerator grant funded.

name: Barvinok-5 Video Module
description: Encrypted digital video replacing analog FPV. FHSS pseudo-random frequency hopping with very small packets. Retains one-directional transmission and low latency of analog. Prototype phase, beginning manufacturer integration.

name: 1001 DJI Military Firmware
description: Custom firmware for DJI Mavic drones. Removes flight limits, improves GPS spoofing resistance, enables high-capacity batteries. ~200,000 drones flashed. Distribution via closed terminal network. Infrastructure compromised by cyberattack July 2025.

name: CIAJeepDoors
description: Disables DJI Remote ID to prevent AeroScope operator tracking. Delivered via USB device (Olga). DJI newer models block this patch.

name: mLRS
description: Open-source LoRa RC + full MAVLink telemetry. 433/868/915/2400 MHz, 7-87 km range. Full diversity. MatekSys purpose-built hardware. ArduPilot native support. Not military-specific but BVLOS/contested-relevant.

name: DroneBridge ESP32
description: ESP32 serial-to-WiFi/ESP-NOW telemetry bridge. AES-256-GCM encryption. MAVLink/MSP/LTM. ~1 km ESP-NOW LR. Swarm via encrypted ESP-NOW broadcasts. $3-5 per board. Pure C (ESP-IDF).
